channel.title: vmware on Jacob Sanford
channel.description: Recent content in vmware on Jacob Sanford

item.title: Deploying a docker-machine to a VMWare VSphere Host
item.description: Docker-Machine and VSphere By default, docker-machine on OSX leverages Virtualbox to deploy a Boot2Docker VM to serve as an endpoint for the host docker commands. This configuration satisfies a common use case for development, but does suffer from drawbacks relating to “the 3 P’s” : Performance, Persistence and Permission issues (due to VBoxSF). You can dodge some of these by provisioning Boot2Docker VM on a vSphere managed host, instead of your local computer.
